summary: A solid early‑69 show highlighted by Jerry’s fledgling pedal‑steel work and standout country‑rock moments; the patched soundboard has a slight wavering pitch and brief equipment hiccups but remains listenable.
review: The concert captures an experimental summer‑1969 set with early versions of Working Man’s Dead material, strong performances of Morning Dew, He Was a Friend of Mine, and a smoking Sitting on Top of the World. While Jerry Garcia’s pedal‑steel is rough around the edges, it adds a unique flavor, and the soundboard source, though patched with some pitch wobble and a short glitch at the start of Lovelight, delivers an overall good listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (high‑energy jams, notable song renditions) and give high star ratings, but several note recording imperfections (pitch wavering, equipment issues) that temper the overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Patched version with a slight pitch wobble and some tuning cuts; brief equipment glitch in the first five minutes of Lovelight; overall clear enough to enjoy the performance.

standout_songs: He Was a Friend of Mine
Sitting on Top of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: A single, dawn‑time set focused on country‑rock and folk material from the Working Man’s Dead era; the band experiments with pedal steel and leans into shorter, more structured songs rather than extended psychedelic jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: First forays on pedal steel—rough but interesting; notable contributions on Morning Dew and vocal work; some equipment hiccups noted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides a sweet accompaniment to Green Green Grass of Home and delivers a Southern‑accented take on Me & My Uncle.
band_member_comments.Drums: Frantic, energetic drumming highlighted especially during Sitting on Top of the World.
